description:
Currently only 'common/' group exists. Agents need more command groups:

1. git group:
   - git status (parse status output)
   - git branch (list branches)
   - git log (recent commits)
   - git diff (unstaged changes)

2. docker group:
   - docker ps (list containers)
   - docker images (list images)
   - docker logs (fetch logs)

3. project group:
   - project init (scaffold project)
   - project lint (run linters)
   - project test (run tests)

4. system group:
   - system process-list
   - system disk-usage
   - system memory-usage

This makes agent-arsenal a more powerful tool for autonomous agents.
title: FEAT: Add more built-in command groups for common agent tasks

description:
Currently aliases are defined in markdown frontmatter but not exposed in CLI help.

Current state:
- uuid.md has 'aliases: [guid]'
- arsenal common guid  # Works! But not shown in help

Expected:
- arsenal common uuid --help  # Should show 'guid' as alias
- Or: arsenal common --help  # Shows all aliases

This helps agents discover all ways to invoke a command.
title: IMPROVE: Add CLI alias support at command level

description:
Agents need to chain commands together.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal common uuid | arsenal common base64 encode
- Or: arsenal common base64 encode --input (arsenal common uuid)

This allows:
1. Generate UUID, then base64 encode it
2. Parse JSON, then extract specific field
3. Build processing pipelines
title: FEAT: Add command chaining support (pipe output to next command)

description:
Session state is lost between CLI invocations. This is critical for agents that need to maintain context.

Reproduction:


Expected: State should persist for session scope within the same agent session.

Fix suggestions:
1. Use environment variable or file-based session state
2. Document clearly that session state is per-process only
3. Provide a PID-based session mechanism for agents
title: BUG: State does not persist between CLI invocations

description:
Different commands use inconsistent argument patterns, confusing for agents:

1. base64: Uses --mode encode/decode but CLI expects 'encode' as separate arg:
   - arsenal common base64 --input 'hello' --mode encode  # Works
   - echo 'hello' | arsenal common base64 encode  # FAILS

2. json: Doesn't have --mode, uses boolean flags:
   - arsenal common code json --input '{}' --validate  # Works
   - No way to specify 'format' mode explicitly

3. hash: Uses --algorithm consistently - good pattern to follow

Recommendation: Standardize on subcommand style:
- arsenal common base64 encode --input 'hello'
- arsenal common base64 decode --input 'aGVsbG8='
- arsenal common code json format --input '{}'
- arsenal common code json validate --input '{}'
title: BUG: Inconsistent argument naming across commands

description:
Agents need to programmatically discover available commands. Currently 'arsenal list' returns 'No such command' error.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal list                # List all available commands
- arsenal list --json         # Machine-readable output
- arsenal list common         # Filter by command group

This is critical for autonomous agents to understand what commands are available.
title: FEAT: Add 'list' subcommand for command discovery

description:
The state management is confusing because session state doesn't persist between CLI calls.

Either:
1. Fix the behavior so state persists across invocations
2. Or document clearly that state is only for:
   - Within a single CLI invocation (programmatic use)
   - Use persistent scope for file-backed state

Current behavior is misleading and causes confusion for agents.

See related issue: agent-arsenal-egb (State does not persist)
title: IMPROVE: Document that state is per-process (not cross-invocation)

close_reason: Dry run feature has been implemented
description:
Agents need safety when running potentially destructive commands.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal state clear --dry-run  # Preview what would be cleared
- arsenal state set key value --dry-run  # Preview what would be set

This allows agents to:
1. Preview effects before executing
2. Get approval from user before destructive changes
3. Log intended actions for debugging
title: FEAT: Add --dry-run flag for destructive commands

close_reason: Implemented --quiet and --no-color flags with OutputManager abstraction
description:
Agents need silent operation for automation.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal --quiet common uuid  # Only output the result
- No banner, no colors, just the result

This is useful for:
1. Piping output to other commands
2. Scripting
3. CI/CD pipelines
title: FEAT: Add --quiet flag for silent operation

description:
Some commands perform expensive operations (e.g., API calls, computations). Agents could benefit from caching.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal common uuid --cache  # Cache result for 5 minutes
- arsenal common uuid --cache-ttl 3600  # Cache for 1 hour
- arsenal cache clear  # Clear all cached results

This helps:
1. Reduce redundant computations
2. Speed up agent workflows
3. Handle rate-limited APIs
title: FEAT: Add result caching for expensive operations

close_reason: Implemented --verbose/-V flag for detailed execution output
description:
Agents need verbose output for debugging and logging.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal --verbose common uuid  # Show execution details
- Output: Executing handler: uuid.handle_uuid
-         Args: {'version': 4, 'count': 1}
-         Result: 0392dc98-b367-4aa2-879f-0bd44935413b

This helps agents:
1. Debug command execution
2. Log detailed traces
3. Understand failures
title: FEAT: Add --verbose/-v flag for detailed execution output

close_reason: Implemented --json flag for machine-readable CLI output. Added json field to OutputConfig, implemented _print_json() and _print_text() methods in OutputManager, added --json flag to CLI, updated print_result() to accept CommandResult, and added unit/integration tests.
description:
Agents need structured output (JSON) for programmatic parsing. Currently all commands return plain text.

Expected behavior:
- arsenal common uuid --json
- Output: {"success": true, "output": "uuid-here", "error": null}

This is essential for agents to:
1. Parse command results programmatically
2. Chain commands together
3. Handle errors gracefully
title: FEAT: Add --json output flag for machine-readable results

description:
Agents need a Python API to execute commands programmatically, not just CLI.

Expected API:


This allows agents to:
1. Import the library and use it in Python scripts
2. Handle results programmatically
3. Build higher-level abstractions
title: FEAT: Add Python API for programmatic command execution

description:
Some error messages don't include helpful recovery steps.

Example:
- Deno sandbox error: 'Deno is not installed' - good!
- But other errors could be more helpful

Improve:
1. JSON parse errors could suggest using --validate flag
2. State errors could explain scope options
3. File not found errors could suggest using 'arsenal list'

This helps agents automatically recover or escalate appropriately.
title: DOCS: Improve error messages with installation instructions
